user_query:
Write a 900–1200-word, publication-ready article based on the materials provided. Read all inputs from: input/brief.json, input/keywords.json, input/sources.md, and input/stats.csv. Then produce these files:

1) output/article.md — Requirements:
- Begin with a question hook in the very first sentence (<= 25 words, end with a question mark).
- Include an H1 headline ("# ") that contains the primary keyword exactly as given in input/keywords.json.
- Use at least three H2 subheadings ("## ") and at least one H3 ("### ").
- Include at least two quoted statements with this exact attribution format and ASCII quotes: "Statement," said Source Name, Title/Organization.
- Incorporate at least one exact statistic phrase from input/stats.csv verbatim.
- Use the primary keyword 6–20 times in total and include the phrase "shadow IT" at least once.
- Keep paragraphs to a maximum of five sentences each.
- End with a "Sources" section that lists exactly five sources chosen from input/sources.md: 3 Primary, 1 Expert, and 1 Secondary. Each source must be listed on its own line in this exact format: "- [ID] Title — Type" using the ID and Type exactly as in input/sources.md.

2) output/seo.json — A JSON object with:
- "title": 50–60 characters long.
- "description": 150–160 characters long.
- "keywords": an array including the primary keyword and at least three secondary keywords taken from input/keywords.json.

3) output/citations.json — A JSON object with a "sources" array listing the same five sources you put in the Sources section (in the same IDs and Types). Each item should have: {"id": "[ID]", "type": "Primary|Expert|Secondary", "title": "Title"}.

Voice and framing:
- Follow a professional digital-business style (news-feature blend) for the audience described in input/brief.json.
- Keep the first 100 words informative, and maintain an objective tone with practical steps.

Do not create any files outside the output/ directory. Make sure all constraints are met, including exact match of any quoted attribution and any copied statistic phrases.
